entry.title: Re: Not able to purchase one/six attempt for SAP certific...
entry.content: Every India people interested by CER001 or CER006 has to create a case?

entry.title: Re: Using APIs to Migrate Incentive Management Commission...
entry.content: Hi Ron - We use the API for migrations. I recommend using the pipeline endpoint with the XMLImport command. For the objects that do not get exported to XML e.g. titles and classifiers we use those respective endpoints. Feel free to reach out if you want more information or a demonstration.

entry.title: Re: Is SQL Anywhere viable and sustainable
entry.content:
You can find this information in

  • PAM Product Availability Matrix Details for Product Version SAP SQL ANYWHERE 17.0 'Unrestricted available' but until end of 2028
  • OSS KBA 2381119 - About the latest version of SQL Anywhere SAP Knowledge Base Article 
  • Online help SAP SQL Anywhere Supported Platforms and Engineering Support Status

entry.title: Re: "Runtime error - Description of Exception". When post...
entry.content:
The error indicates that it's trying to make an RFC call to GTS, but the destination in question does not exist.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Passing Bas64 Encoded string of a PDF as Email attachment from Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Jibin, Thanks for reaching out to us, 

1. Direct Usage of Base64 Content for Email Attachment

Most email services or APIs (including the ones integrated into SAP BPA) support attachments in Base64 format. You can directly use the Base64 string in your email task or workflow.

  • Steps:

    1. Ensure the Base64 string you fetch from AWS S3 is valid and includes the MIME type.
    2. In the BPA email action, add the Base64 string as an attachment.
    3. Specify the MIME type (application/pdf) and filename explicitly.

  • Example Configuration for Email Task:

    • Attachment Data: The Base64 string from S3.
    • Attachment Name: The filename (e.g., document.pdf).
    • Attachment MIME Type: application/pdf.

This avoids storing the file locally or using DMS, as the Base64 content is passed directly.

entry.title: Date prefilled as Yesterday in Calender
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am looking out for a calender to be appearing on the top of the SAC story with yesterday's date selected and if today is Monday then it should have selected last friday. Is there any way we can acheieve it in story filter.

Regards,

Singh

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Analytic Model oData: 404 Resource Not Found
entry.content: Hmm, while calling the API you would try Developer Tools (Ctrl+Shift+I) in your browser and monitor Console and Network, etc. It may give you some clue for the cause!

entry.title: How to DISABLE the Presentation Mode in SAC?
entry.content:
Hello,

Does anyone know how to DISABLE the new functionality - presentation mode in SAP SAC? It happens to be confusing for our users and the comments don't get saved when they enter in that mode. So we want to disable it. It's useless for us.

SAP Analytics Cloud, hybrid analytics

entry.title: Re: Populate "Created by" and "Created on" in the Input r...
entry.content: Hello Surbhi, Thank you for reaching out to us. Have you had a chance to review the solution proposed by Gregor? If you need further clarification or have additional details to share, please let us know so we can investigate further.

entry.title: Value Driver Tree drill down - SAC design
entry.content:
Hello, 

I've created a simple VDT in an optimized story.

 

Drill down to customers/sku works well.

 

However, every attempt to drill up or collapse fails, and I lose the first aggregated design - I'm always getting back to the customers I've selected.

example no1 - collapse from the revenue will just hide/reveal the customers I've selected

example no2: trying to collapse the European customers - it collapses only the European rebates

 

 

 

 

 

 

When is this bug expected to be corrected?

Deb

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation Subcription Failed
entry.content:
Hi Sayanasatyarpa,

Thank you for reaching out to us.

On the error screen, if you click on "More Details," you should see the reason for the failure. In the meantime, please ensure that the entitlement is enabled and all prerequisites for SAP BPA are met.

Feel free to update us if you continue to face any issues.

entry.title: Delivery Output Determination in shipment document missing
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have an issue regards the output determination in the shipment document.

Pratically, When I create the shipment document the system automatically link the output message type to the shipment. The problem is that when I link the delivery document to the shipment, the output message type of the Ship-to automatically disappear.

Can anyone help me? Maybe I miss something or I don't know.

Thanks

Bye

entry.title: Re: /SCWM/RWEIGHT_VOLUME_UPDATE in Public Edition
entry.content: Hi Clark, You are correct. We found the same root cause. It appears we loaded our bins before the warehouse attributes were transported to the Test system. Our Productive system has not yet been issued and will ensure the correct sequence is followed for this load. If the correction report does not work, we can the re-load data. Thank you for your time.

entry.title: Re: Enable Now Producer Functionalities
entry.content:
Hello Romina, most of your questions are answered in the SAP Enable Now Info Center website at https://enable-now.sap.com . It's free to sign up with your email or SAP ID. 

  • Are there standard templates available in the producer or does the customer needs to create custom templates for E-Learnings? - There are a few standard Book Styles with clickable buttons available in the Resources of your workarea. You can also build your own Book Style. You can designate any Book Page (slide) as a Book Page Template in its Properties, and control the appearance of your Book Pages with the Book Page Template. You can download more Book Styles and Book Page Templates from the SAP Enable Now Info Center. Most of my customers build their own. 
  • Is there any example scorm content generated with the producer to show to the customer and test the integration with their LMS? - No, you need to build a Book (a single Sharable Content Object/SCO), with a couple of Book Pages, a Simulation with all the playback modes you intend to use, and maybe a Quiz. Then publish it as whatever flavor of SCORM you need, and test it in your LMS. The results are highly variable depending on your content, your SCORM version, and your LMS. You can also go to SCORM.com (run by Rustici Software), and download a sample SCORM package for testing. You can also test your own SCORM packages there and get automated feedback on what's wrong with your package. 
  • Are there functions like interacting persons who can be inserted in the book pages? - if you mean Avatars, yes, the Comic Man and Comic Woman (editable avatars) are in your Resources. You can download others, like Dotty, or a series of non-editable avatars, from the SAP Enable Now Info Center website. Most of my customers don't use avatars.

You can purchase SAP Enable Now implementation assistance through SAP or its SEN-certified partners. Contact your SAP salesperson for information. The SEN implementation consultant will help you set up your SEN, configure it for what you want to do, and guide you in the proper use and care of SEN.

entry.title: Datasphere Task Chain Mail Notifications are not working
entry.content:
Herkese merhaba

Aşağıdaki gibi alt görev zincirlerini içeren bir ana görev zinciri oluşturdum: 

İşlem başarıyla veya başarısızlıkla tamamlandığında bana bildirimde bulunması için hem alt zincirler hem de ana zincir için e-posta bildirimleri ayarladım. Ancak, başarılı veya başarısız olduğunda e-posta göndermiyor.


Bir Görev Zinciri başarısız bir şekilde tamamlandığında ve e-posta gönderilemediğinde oluşan hatayla ilgili bir SAP Notu buldum:  SAP Notu 0003440103  Bu Not, başarısız görev zinciri görevleri için Datasphere'de bir hata olduğunu belirtir.
Başarılı Görev Zinciri görevleri için de benzer bir durum var mı, yoksa eksik bir e-posta yapılandırması mı var yoksa başka bir sorun mu var?
Aylar önce görev zincirini ilk denediğimde aldığım e-posta bildiriminin ekran görüntüsünü gönderiyorum.

Bilgisi olan varsa bilgi verirse sevinirim.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build App - Multiple Dates Field alignment
entry.content:
Hi Anam, 

the issue of date fields not being fully visible despite resolving the overlapping with Z-index could be due to constraints in the container size or padding/margin settings. Here are a few suggestions to address this:

  1. Adjust Container Size:
    Ensure the container holding the date fields is large enough to accommodate the full content of the fields. You can do this by increasing the width and height of the container.

  2. Padding and Margin Settings:
    Review the padding and margin settings of the container and the date fields. Sometimes, insufficient spacing can cause clipping. Add extra padding or margin to ensure the fields have enough room to display fully.

  3. Field Alignment:
    Verify the alignment settings for the date fields. Set them to "Center" or "Stretch" as per your layout requirements to ensure they occupy the available space properly.

  4. Scaling or Font Adjustment:
    Check the font size and scaling of the date fields. Reducing the font size slightly might help fit the content without needing large gaps.

  5. Responsive Layout:
    If the app is responsive, test it on different screen sizes. Adjust the layout settings (e.g., flex or grid layout) to ensure the date fields are visible across various devices.

  6. Inspect Element Styles:
    Use the debugging tools (like the developer console in the browser) to inspect the styles applied to the fields. Look for overflow, clip, or hidden properties that might be causing the issue and adjust them.

If the issue persists after applying these suggestions, please provide additional details, such as screenshots or the layout setup, for further assistance.

entry.title: Re: Vendor Payment Process After Item Is Sold
entry.content:
Hi

follow-up questions.

1. For the retail industry with multiple stores, how can consignment stock be effectively managed across various locations?

It can be managed as per sites and stores, so you can transfer stock to respective sites or stores. 

2. If a customer returns an item after the invoice has been submitted, how can it be differentiated within the consignment process?

Make a agreement to payment to vendor with time line to customer problem so you will get a gap to see whether it is a customer returns or not ( but it is a challenge to deal)

3. Regarding sales quantities, should we rely on reports, or is there a way to integrate this directly into the consignment process?

As per my knowledge, It is better to transfer to own premises using 411 and then need to sell it

4. Are there any best practices for managing consignment settlement and tracking in a retail environment?

Really That you need to explore. I have not checked it . I am sorry to it 

 

Regards

Rashid

I know that ASE 16.1 is being released Q1 2025 and SP04 PL07 is being released Q2 2025, but will there be any more patch releases for 16.0 after SP04 PL07?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Language of Folders
entry.content: Hi @Stephen_Niemann, as you were referring to the folder names and diagram titles, I am afraid that Kristof's answer (from the Support Team) is the valid one. I made the false assumption that the same rules apply here as for the language settings for the menu/labels in the application, but unfortunately this is not the case. Sorry for the confusion.

entry.title: Re: Talent Intelligence Hub - Myths and Misconceptions - Part 1 (Prerequisites)
entry.content: @Avinash9 Have you run the Check Tool in all areas connected to the Job Description Manager and Job Profile Builder (including any associated mappings)? If any errors result, they need to be resolved prior to attempting the migration/upgrade to TIH, otherwise it will fail.

entry.title: Do you know the key components, functionalities, and real-world applications of SAP Order Management
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Exploring SAP Order Management Foundation and Sourcing & Availability learning journey.

Overview
This course provides comprehensive insights into the key components, functionalities, and real-world applications of SAP Order Management. It covers foundational concepts, omni-channel fulfillment challenges, and customer-centric use cases to enhance your skills in SAP Order Management solutions.

Learning objectives
By the end of this course, you will be able to:

  • Identify and address key challenges in omni-channel order management.
  • Understand and utilize the core functionalities of SAP Order Management Foundation.
  • Apply order orchestration concepts and manage order activities & events.
  • Integrate analytics to gain business insights.
  • Explore functionalities for sourcing and availability management.
  • Analyze and implement best practices from various customer use cases.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 
I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Recurring Process and Multi-Selection of Activities in Process Scheduler
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!  

Today marks the last blogpost for the SAP Profitability and Performance Management (SAP PaPM) Cloud – Standard Model for the year 2024.  

As we have introduced the Process Scheduler during Q3 of 2022 in this blogpost, Process Scheduler: Execution made easy by Scheduling a “Job” in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud, we are pleased to announce the new enhancements of the Process Scheduler.  

Previously, a scheduled job in Process Scheduler could run a specific activity in a process template or process instance at a specific date and time. But great news everyone! Due to the requests of our customers, I am delighted to announce that our Process Scheduler can now handle recurring processes to which you can schedule a job that will run activities every day, every hour, every month, and so on. Yes! You’ve read it right. A job can now handle multiple activities in a process template or process instance that will run sequentially. 

How will it work? Let’s dive right in.  

In adding a new Job or copying through Job From, there are 2 new fields in “Add process schedule” dialogue, namely Repeat Interval and End time.  

1. Repeat Interval – In this field, you can specify the interval for the job to repeat. You can enter a number followed by a time unit such as 2 days, 1 week, 3 months, etc. For instance, if you enter “2 days”, the job will be triggered every 2 days at the scheduled launch time. Only numbers and English time units (for example, days, weeks, months) are currently being accepted.  

Note: This feature uses the SAP Job Scheduling Service. For detailed instructions on entering the repeat interval correctly, see Human-Readable Date Formats section in SAP Job Scheduling Service Cloud documentation.  

2. End time – This field will be enabled once “Repeat Interval” is maintained. In this section, you can set an end date and time to when the recurring runs will stop. If this is left blank, it will also be accepted and will run recurrently that will only stop by deleting the job created.  

3. Activities - This field is where we can select 1 or more Execution Activities in a process template or process instance that we would choose to run sequentially based on the order of selection made.  

After confirmation, the information about the job created will be displayed on top of the list in the left-hand side panel. Let’s highlight the column header “Next planned launch”, which was previously named “Planned launch”, that will show when the next run recurrence is happening. In our case, this is the first run. Also, there is a new column heading introduced here, which is the “Repeat Interval”.  

Now let’s focus on the right-hand side panel.  

Validation of the execution status of the recurring processes can be checked under “Runs” located in the right-hand side panel.  

Runs – Displays information about each job run. You can see the sequence number of the currently displayed job run in relation to a total number of scheduled job runs (for example 1/2 or 2/2), and you can switch between them using the arrows next to the job count. 

Now, I will show you an example of how it works. In this example, we will schedule a job that will run 2 activities every 5 minutes with a total of 4 Runs.  

After confirming, the system will create a job, which will appear at the top of the list of scheduled jobs with the status “SCHEDULED”. The panel on the right will show more detailed information about the job run.  

After the 1st successful run, the next planned launch will be changed to the next scheduled recurrence and the state will still be in “SCHEDULED” status. The Runs will create another page below the scheduled Job run page (2/2). 

After the 2nd successful run, the next planned launch will be changed to the next scheduled recurrence and the state will still be in “SCHEDULED” status. The Runs will create another page below the scheduled job run 2/3.  

After the 3rd successful run, the next planned launch will be changed to the next scheduled recurrence and the state will still be in “SCHEDULED” status. The Runs will create another page below the scheduled job run.   

After the 4th successful run, since it will be the last recurrence, the state of the job will now be set to “COMPLETED” and all the runs are now set to ”COMPLETED”. 

And there you have it. Like and share this blog post with your colleagues who may be interested in this new feature. Watch out for future enhancements and improvements of the Process Scheduler. This information is also available in our Application Help

Thank you and Happy holidays!

entry.title: Re: Build and Run Multi-Tenant SaaS Solutions on the SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi Knut,

I am trying to run a few examples of CAP applications, but I am messed in many new terms.

What is difference between multi-tenant, multi-target and multi-backend applications/solutions? Is it the same thing or something else? Is it multi-target format outdated?

I managed to develop a few simple Fiori apps on base List Report Page template with header/items and actions. At least there is a good tutorial ("Build a Business Application Using CAP for Node.js") how to develop and deploy so-called "multi-target" CAP apps. It works anyway. Nevertheless I am stuck with getting access to my data from an external app (from Postman, for example). The Poetry Slams (partner-reference-application) tutorial has a frightening size and contents a lot of redundant things for us. I tried to perform all steps of "42a Enable API Access to SAP BTP Applications Using Service Broker" topic but the broker service failed with crash during the deployment. Another tutorial "Secure a Basic Node.js App with the Authorization and Trust Management Service (XSUAA)" does not work either - it is not entirely clear how to just run the "Product List" app in Business Application Studio. At least the "cds watch" way does not work. I would wish to see something more like a "hello, world" example. Could you please recommend some simpler tutorial on authorization and OData API, probably without the multi-tenant feature?

Thank you.

Aleksei Belousov

entry.title: Re: Number Range for Serial Number Profile
entry.content: How to connect serial number range with serial number profile so that it will be created during production order release?

entry.title: Re: "Runtime error - Description of Exception". When posting PGI from VL02N
entry.content:
Note carefully 'No RFC destination is defined for SAP Global Trade Services' (missing configuration in this system?)

  • Read KBA 2127646 - KBA: No RFC destination is defined for SAP Global Trade Services Message no. /SAPSLL/PLUGINR3002
  • If GTS actually required, read 2447201 - KBA: Where to find GTS Documentation

entry.title: SAP Community Call for ALM
entry.content:
The free monthly webinar provides everything you need to know about SAP's Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) offerings. Get the latest news directly from SAP's topic experts!

With the monthly SAP Community Call for ALM, you have the chance to stay updated about what's going on with SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Focused Run.

For slides and recordings click here.

Please register here!

entry.title: Re: Approver workflow for standard fiori application
entry.content:
Hello Vijay, Thanks for reaching out to us, 

es, it is technically possible to integrate a business approval workflow into a standard Fiori application like the "Manage Cost Centers (Version 2)" (F1443A) app while adhering to a clean core approach. Here’s how you can achieve this:

1. Extend the Standard Fiori App Using Adaptation

SAP provides tools like UI Adaptation and In-App Extensions in SAP S/4HANA, which allow adding custom fields or logic without modifying the core application. Use these tools to introduce a mechanism where the creation process triggers a workflow.

2. Business Workflow Integration

  • Workflow Trigger: Implement a pre-check mechanism that triggers the workflow when a new cost center is about to be created. This can be done using BAdIs (Business Add-Ins) or Event-Driven Architecture (e.g., CAP or RAP-based events) to invoke the workflow.

  • Approval Workflow: Create an approval workflow in SAP Build Process Automation 

    • Input: Pass the requestor information and app ID (F1443A) as part of the workflow payload.
    • Logic: Use SAP Business Rules service to determine the approver based on the input data (e.g., app ID and requestor).
    • Approval Task: Route the approval task to the SAP My Inbox app of the approver.

entry.title: Create attachment (photo) with personas on SE on mobile doesn't work
entry.content:
Hello,

I've created a flavor to create attachment on purchase requisition via the camera.

It's working fine on pc on windows with a browser.

On Android mobile, first I get this error (see image), that I don't get on pc.

When I continue, I can open the camera, then on firefox when trying to confirm the upload, I get a dump: "Exception condition "X_ERROR" triggered".

If I switch to Chrome browser, I don't get the dump but same error message.

Thanks,

Emmanuel.

entry.title: Re: SAP Content Server 7.53 Installation and Connectivity with ERP using SSL
entry.content:
Hello, SAP do not recommend to use "/sapcs" but "sapcs" only for HTTP Script field.

When the URL is generated it will compose it based on the data of OAC0 Content Repository.
So this is basically telling the runtime to user http://myhost:1090//sapcs.... there will be an additional "/" character with the given configuration in your Blog.

Furthermore, on the CSADMIN settings.
You are using on section "5. Post-Configurations:" on the 3rd screenshot, you are using Database parameters mixed up with FileSystem storage type. This is incorrect.

The correct is to use "StorageDriver" for FSStorage and no further DB parameter references.

StorageDriver FSStorage
ContRepRoot X:\usr\sap\SCS
Security 1
ContRepDescription SAP CS New installation

 

Parameters are described on this SAP Note.
329473 - Description of Content Server and Cache Server configuration file

entry.title: Year-end closing for non-leading ledger in ECC
entry.content:
This is about the Balance carry forward customer/vendor (F.07) for non-leading ledger .

The Fiscal year for non-leading ledger is from Jan to Dec and FY for leading ledger is April to March. My question is how to perform the balance carry forward process for vendor/customer (F.07) for non-leading ledger in Dec to get the report correctly for Local reporting since this process is not ledger specific. 

Once the next fiscal year (say 2025) is opened for non-leading ledger, will the values of leading ledger reflect automatically in non-leading or how...?

Thanks in advance.

Balam

entry.title: Re: Templates on Disclosure Management
entry.content: Great work Rodrigo! Useful topic.

entry.title: Re: difference in fs10n and faglb03
entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: Re: difference in fs10n and faglb03
entry.content: Hello, could you please let me know, whether FS10N is selecting from GLTo and FAGLB03 is selecting from FAGLFLEXT? Regards, Wolfgang

entry.title: Re: Long time to run a report in VS2022 the first time
entry.content:
What SP are you using? What changed in the past 3 weeks? look for Windows patch history, has been known to cause problems.

SP 37 was just released:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

See my Blog on upgrading:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/upgrading-a-visual-studio-2008-gt-2022-net-project-with-the-latest-cr-net/ba-p/13469215

I don't know why but it is normal for the first report to take more time due to the app loading all of the CR runtime.

It can also take time if you have any Verify DB options enabled in the Report Options, uncheck if not required.

Often it is recommended to load a dummy report when the app first starts up to preload the CR runtime.

entry.title: Re: Remove the share button from my SAP Fiori Element app...

entry.title: Issue with merging SFCs in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi DM Experts,

I used the SFC Split function to split a SFC into two SFCs, both linked to the same order. Following this, I attempted to merge the two SFCs back together using the SFC Merge function, but I received the following error message:

"Merge across operations is not possible on the standard routing."

 

 

Scenario Details

  • Split Operation: The original SFC was successfully split into two SFC

  • Routing Plan: Both SFCs are linked to a order-specific routing 

  • SFC Status: Both SFCs are currently in the "in the Queue" status

  • Operation: Both SFCs are assigned to the same operation at the same resource

Thank you in advance for your support!

Best regards,
Gizem

entry.title: Is there any way to restrict access of T-codes involved in FIORI standard catalogs for VA01 for SOD
entry.content:
Is it possible to restrict access to specific transactions within a standard catalog? Currently, we need to limit access to the VA01 transaction code in the standard catalog SAP_SD_BC_SO_PROC_OP, which contains multiple transactions such as VA01, VA02, and VA03.

I attempted to create a new catalog to achieve this, but I couldn't maintain groups in the new catalog. The system is connected to ECC and Fiori.

Could you guide me on whether there is a way to restrict access to only VA01 in ECC or through another approach?

entry.title: Re: Mismatch in CJI5/Table Commitment Values & PR value
entry.content: Run transaction CJBN to rebuild budget availability.  You can also run CJEN to rebuild the PS Info structure.  It's a good idea to schedule these transactions to run periodically.

entry.title: Companion links appearing in multiple apps.
entry.content:
We built SAP Companion links within one app, but they are appearing on multiple apps within Fiori. We tried deleting and re-adding the link, but the problem persists. We verified our Manager settings and everything looks correct. 

 

  • SAP FIORI FOR SAP S/4HANA 2023
  • SEN Cloud Edition 10.8.9 (build 5711)

entry.title: Hotkey Macro for SAP Applications
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m looking for software recommendations to help automate some repetitive tasks in my workflow. Specifically, I need a tool that can:

  • Control programs through automatic text inputs or mouse clicks.
  • Book predefined materials to a project.
  • Book work activities (A, B, or C) of an employee (variable employee X, Y, or Z) to projects.

Since the materials, employees, and activities are always the same, I would like to store these in a table as variables and retrieve them with a hotkey.

In Past we used AutoHotkey for this Szenario. But due to some policy issues we are looking for an alternative Software. Is there an SAP product or external?

Any suggestions for software that can handle these requirements would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Error when modifying SY-SUBRC in S/4HANA Cloud Public...

entry.title: Re: SAP IQ 16.1 - Which tools can you recommend to track...
entry.content: Grafana can send alerts by e-mail: Grafana: Configure email for Alerting

entry.title: Shaping the Future: SAP Analytics Cloud as a Resilient Force in the AI-Driven Evolution of Business
entry.content:
The Artificial Intelligence Storm

In today’s rapidly evolving technological world, businesses face the dual challenge of staying competitive while navigating through disruptive trends like artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML). Many platforms face the risk of obsolescence as they struggle to keep pace with changing demands. Nevertheless, despite these transformation trends, SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) continues to prove its relevance by adapting and integrating into a dynamic ecosystem and landscapes of the companies and benefits from a continuous commitment to improvement and adoption. SAC remains a vital tool for organizations that are seeking to derive value from their data, as it is core to SAP’s product portfolio focusing on driving future innovation.

Evolving with the AI Revolution

SAC’s Integration and Privileges in the AI Storm

AI has fundamentally shifted the way organizations approach business intelligence (BI) and analytics. Traditional dashboards and manual reports are being enhanced or even fully replaced by predictive and augmented analytics and conversational BI tools. Companies are increasingly demanding platforms capable not only of analyzing historical data but also of anticipating trends and suggesting actionable insights in real time.

SAP Analytics Cloud is not just adapting to these demands but thrives to represent such an environment itself. It embeds machine learning and AI-driven analytics directly into its core functionalities and as a result, empowers its users to go beyond descriptive analytics. For example, its predictive planning capabilities allow businesses to simulate scenarios and to plan for outcomes with the highest accuracy. SAC supports other wide range of functionalities, such as "augmented analytics," which enables  AI to guide users through complex datasets and highlight patterns

Apart from the functionalities, one of SAP Analytics Cloud’s strongest assets is its seamless integration into SAP’s product ecosystem. Businesses that are already leveraging SAP solutions—be it SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, or SAP Ariba—find that SAC fits naturally into their workflows. This integration ensures a unified data landscape where users can access real-time insights without additional challenges or fragmented datasets.

SAC acts as a bridge between on-premises and cloud environments, allowing businesses to adopt hybrid analytics strategies. This flexibility is particularly critical for companies in industries with strict compliance requirements or those undergoing cloud transformations.

By remaining deeply embedded within the SAP portfolio, SAC reinforces its relevance. As organizations adopt new AI-driven tools and methodologies, SAC’s ability to integrate with emerging technologies ensures it evolves in parallel with the broader business environment.

Furthermore, SAP’s roadmap for SAC emphasizes agility, with regular updates and new features designed to align with emerging trends. This ensures that customers not only have access to state-of-the-art analytics but also a sense of security that their investment will remain relevant in the years to come. SAC is a dynamic platform, built to evolve and adjust in harmony with its users' needs.

Looking Ahead

The future of analytics is undeniably intertwined with Artificial Intelligence. Yet, the need for robust, reliable, and integrated BI tools remains unchanged. SAP Analytics Cloud exemplifies how a platform can adapt to technological shifts while remaining a dependable solution for businesses.

As the AI era progresses, SAC will continue to evolve, delivering actionable insights, fostering collaboration, and driving business outcomes. For organizations seeking a future-proof BI solution, SAC’s deep integration with SAP products and commitment to innovation makes it a natural choice

By focusing on integration, usability, and technological evolution, SAP Analytics Cloud is poised to remain at the forefront of analytics innovation, ensuring that businesses can thrive in an ever-changing world.

 

Andrii Pogorielov, Seda Agasarjan

Customer Services & Delivery MEE BT&A CFO Advisory

entry.title: Can´t find the output/PDF of a Loading Instruction I´ve printed on the Warehouse Outbound Processing
entry.content:
Hello!
I´m doing test scripts/scope items of the Cloud Public Edition.
On the 3BS, Warehouse Outbound Processing, I can´t find the PDF to check the output of the Loading Instruction printed on the step 4.8 Print Loading Instruction (Optional). Does anyone have a clue on how to check/see that?

*At first I thought its because I don´t have the TM. But the step doesn´t say its only available for TM (some others on this very test script say its required some TM things). But the 4.8 Print Loading Instruction (Optional) doesn´t have a note on that regard. So I am assuming its not related to that.

Best Regards

entry.title: Possible Issue with SAP Fiori Generator v1.16.0 (OData V4 Service Detection)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm experiencing unexpected behavior with the SAP Fiori Generator (@sap/generator-fiori version 1.16.0) in the SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) when trying to create a List Report application based on an OData V4 UI service. Even though I'm selecting an OData V4 service, the generator seems to be defaulting to V2 templates, causing the generated application to render only a blank page.

I've verified that the OData V4 service is accessible and valid. Has anyone else encountered this issue or identified a potential solution?

Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Default Company Code in S/4HANA Public Cloud Organiza...
entry.content:
Hello @Guanyo ,

I guess you encountered that question in setting up the org structure in CBC. Don't worry about that.

In your S/4HANA system, for example, the Dev system, under your profile, there is a Setting for company code. You can see the attached screenshot.  That setting does matter to the user at the end.

Regards,

George

entry.title: Re: Is there option to avoid the popup No routing found while creating production order
entry.content: Check the note 2566699

entry.title: PaPM Early Exit Check
entry.content:
Hello Friends,

we are having trouble with the iterative view of SAP PaPM. I will specify the model we created: 

Let I_RANG be our iteration parameter.  let P_RANG be the rank in the table. i also defined the :I_RANG as a parameter. first i join two tables together  as below:

I have the writer function and put the join function above as input:

Next i have a view function with this writer function as input. i fill the field P_ABORT:

the field P_ABORT has the following formula:

so when the field MAX_RANG, which ist the maximum of P_RANG equals the iteration run, it will be filled with x.

In the advanced tab of the view i have the following EXIT as early exit check:

In the environment tab i defined EXIT as when P_ABORT=x:

It seems that when i let the iterative view run, it runs longer than i specified in the early exit check. so the early exit check does not work.  Does s.o have experience with iterative views? if you need further specification let me know. 

Any help would be appreciated. maybe @ZYT

Best regards, Philipp

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements / CAP application together with SAP C4C ODATA API - Draft Handling Required?
entry.content:
Hi! @thomas_jung In my work experience we have got few cases before where CAP would be a perfect solution for apps based on remote service V4 metadata, but that isn't possible because remote service has no isActiveEntity and other draft properties, so it becomes cumbersome task to use SAP Fiori Elements.
Are there any developments and/ or solutions how to use SAP CAP together with remote V4 services?

Thank you,

Shanir

entry.title: Re: SAP ALM version 2408
entry.content:
Hi, BilalHashmi,
I believe the screen you are on is in Cloud ALM's Implementation > Manage Scopes for "Finance" scope and you are wondering how to adjust the Solution Scenario to use SAP Best Practices for S/4HANA in version 2408.

If this is correct, you will click the Edit button that is in your screen shot.  You should see a screen similar to this which has many entries, including 2408:

Enable the entries you want to use in your Scope definition.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: Issue with Employee History Salary Field for Hourly P...
entry.content: @xavierlegarrec : Can you pls confirm if the patch is also relevant for Compensation review and not just variable pay ?

entry.title: Is it possible to have both gATP and aATP working in the same environment?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Currently I am working in a project where we are moving the old system to the cloud. Part of the scope is to switch from gATP to aATP. However, I have some concerns on how to proceed during the period of time where both would be needed.

Actually, this will be implemented first for a region and later phased in for the other region. The problem is that although they are run in different servers and will have they own SAP systems, they will both share the DEV system meaning in DEV we need to have both aATP and gATP. 

I understand that aATP is activated at Availability Checking Groups level so I was thinking to create new groups specific for aATP so I can easily switch on the aATP functionality. However, I still have reservations in what other implications this shared environment would have and especially related to all the enhancements that are linked to gATP.

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: System asking to enter Access Key when creating class
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I have installed Sap Erp 750 and I've successfully created a custom package starting with Z something like ZREST using transactio Se80 and then using Segw i created a custom project using the defined package and the name is ZREST_ADDRESS_API and i have defined the entity Address and when I try to generte runtime objecta I can see a popup asking an access key? 

What can I do in this case.

Elena

hi

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM / Memory Consumption Tuning for Health...
entry.content:
Hi, Benoit,
To answer your question about:
"How long should I wait before the cleaning of the Health Monitoring would be
performed?"

So long as the Framework-DataReorg job is running, housekeeping should occur once a day -> for more information on this, see KBA 3416639 - How to configure the data retention (housekeeping) in SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring.

(I know you have created a case for the remaining problem of memory consumption not clearing.  Proceed there for the rest of your question.)
Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Output Management in TM - BRF+ or PPF?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

We recently implemented TM but only the basic settings since the client is still not ready to move fully from LE to TM. However, they are looking to step by step leave more room for TM to handled things. On of these is the output management.

Currently, they are using the old NACE to determine the output types and to send the forms and we are discussing to move this to TM. However, I am struggling to understand if this can also be handled via BRF+ or if it needs to go through PPF. I have been reading a lot and it seems for TM the only way to process outputs is via PPF.

On the output control settings, I see the application object types available to be activated limited mostly to Sales Documents, Purchasing Documents, etc but nothing related to Shipments.  Did I understood correctly?

Regards,

In Build Apps:I am using an Application using with connection to SAP on Web Deployed Version.
What would be the way (elegant) to notice that the session has expired and that we can't make calls to back-end anymore before reloading ? 
Thank you

Thanks 

Raghu

entry.title: Re: Is possible to use Tricentis Tosca in SAP CALM?
entry.content:
Hi, LucasBeccon,

I am not exactly sure I understand your question.  SAP Cloud ALM > Implementation does allow a Tricentis connection to the Tricentis Test Automation Tool and allows your users to navigate to Tricentis to create your automated test cases and work there in that UI.

If you check: https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/implementation/sap-cloud-alm-implementation-expert-portal/testmanagement.html?anchorId=section_copy > you will find several resources for information on:

Tricentis Test Automation

Help Portal - Tricentis Test Automation
Documentation - Tricentis Test Automation
Presentation: Tricentis Test Automation for SAP integrated with SAP Cloud ALM

I hope this helps you arrive to your answer question.
 
Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: Error in FB75 while raising to E-Invoice
entry.content: With some luck you could find a solution in 3108760 - eDocument Saudi Arabia Troubleshooting Guide - answer D1 (identification of original invoice?)

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - Possible to recover deleted documents?
entry.content:
Hi,  kjetiltynes,

Your question is very generic and SAP Cloud ALM is quite complex offering many use cases and different areas.  It would help if your post were more specific on exactly what documents you are looking to recover in order to engage someone familiar with that specific area.
With that being said, I know of no recycle bin nor archiving process that might allow you to recover deleted 'elements' in Cloud ALM.
In case you have a request for changes to this area, you can check currently logged requests in our Influence Campaign and up-vote any relevant to your request or you can feel free to add your ideas:

SAP Cloud ALM influence campaigns (ECRs) can be logged at:

If you have a request for a feature/function that is not presently available, you can submit it at:

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Operations https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525

Best regards,
Paula

Tks,

Rosette

entry.title: how can I prevent to change PO price when an agreement price exists ?
How to Retrieve Warehouse Items with Serial Numbers and Supplier Information?
entry.content:
EN

Hi,

I want to do a query that will get all the items on a warehouse and will show the serial number if it exists and also the supplier, date when it was bought, price of the item and DocNum. The issue is that although I have the query that shows the items on the warehouse with the serial number I don't know how to relate this to the other tables to see the supplier information. 

ES

Hola,

Quiero realizar una consulta que obtenga todos los artículos de un almacén y muestre el número de serie si existe, además del proveedor, la fecha de compra, el precio del artículo y el número de documento. El problema es que, aunque tengo la consulta que muestra los artículos del almacén con el número de serie, no sé cómo relacionarlo con las otras tablas para ver la información del proveedor.

For now I have the following query:

 

SELECT 
    T2."ItemCode", 
    T2."ItemName", 
    T1."WhsCode", 
    T1."WhsName",
    IFNULL((SELECT (SUM(Y."InQty") - SUM(Y."OutQty"))
            FROM OINM Y 
            WHERE Y."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode" AND Y."Warehouse" = T1."WhsCode"), 0) AS "Stock",
    IFNULL((SELECT (SUM(Y."TransValue")) 
            FROM OINM Y 
            WHERE Y."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode" AND Y."Warehouse" = T1."WhsCode"), 0) AS "Costo Total",
    T3."ItmsGrpNam",
    T2."LastPurPrc" AS "UltimoPrecioCompra",
    T0."AvgPrice" AS "PrecioPromedio",
    O0."DistNumber" AS "SerialNumber"
FROM 
    OITM T2
    INNER JOIN OITW T0 ON T0."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode"
    INNER JOIN OWHS T1 ON T1."WhsCode" = T0."WhsCode"
    INNER JOIN OITB T3 ON T2."ItmsGrpCod" = T3."ItmsGrpCod"
    LEFT JOIN OSRN O0 
        ON O0."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode" 
        AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM OSRQ O2 
                    WHERE O2."ItemCode" = O0."ItemCode" 
                    AND O2."SysNumber" = O0."SysNumber" 
                    AND O2."WhsCode" = '[%0]') 
WHERE 
    (SELECT (SUM(Y."InQty") - SUM(Y."OutQty")) 
     FROM OINM Y  
     WHERE Y."ItemCode" = T2."ItemCode" 
       AND Y."Warehouse" = T1."WhsCode") != 0
    AND T0."WhsCode" = '[%0]'
ORDER BY 
    T2."ItemCode", 
    T1."WhsCode";

entry.title: Re: Enhanced Error Handling within SAP Digital Manufacturing Production Processes
entry.content:
Hello, Manoel,

Thanks for the blog. I could not find more recent information about this topic, just to confirm that this is the current state of the art.

Is it posible already to achieve what @scb-schozs mentioned on their comment, as in catching not only the error code but also the error message from the service?

There are cases where the services handle not only error code but error messages in a proper way. Specially when there are errors with the same come but the message can differ.

Since the service itslef handles properly the different execptions, it would make more sense to map this information direclty as Service Output, instead of doing another handling at Production Process level, with less capability of mapping detail.

Thanks for your support on this topic again,

Juan.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - "Transport of Copy" - Job Schedule?
entry.content:
Hi, Brad_saxon,

Hopefully you have the answer to this question already, but in case not, I can tell you the job /SDF/CALM_CDM_TR_PROC_CL_DEP-100 is the one that creates transport requests and Transport of Copies (ToCs) in the managed system.  Here is an image showing what you can expect to see in the log for a ToC creation:

and for a transport request:

 

Please find detailed configuration and Change Deployment Management (CDM) information pertaining to background jobs, etc, available in the blog linked within KBA 3468539 - First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher)

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Analytics Average Calculation
entry.content:
Hi User Please check below points 


Verify the Calculated Measure Settings:

Go to the Calculated Measure settings.
Ensure that:
Operation is set to Average.
Aggregation Dimension is correctly set to Region.
Double-check that the measure used for the calculation is numeric and supports aggregation.
Review Chart Configuration:

Open the chart settings and ensure:
The chart type is appropriate for displaying averages (e.g., Bar, Column).
No conflicting aggregations are applied at the chart level.
The measure shown is the calculated measure you created.
Check the Model’s Aggregation Behavior:

Navigate to the Model in SAC and inspect the aggregation behavior of the base measure (e.g., Profit).
The default aggregation of the base measure (sum, average, etc.) should not conflict with the calculated measure.
Set Aggregation in the Chart:

In the chart designer:
Select the calculated measure.
Ensure that the aggregation is explicitly set to Average at the chart level.

Filter Settings:

Ensure that the filters applied to the chart or story do not inadvertently affect the calculation (e.g., Region filters might lead to unintended aggregation).
Use the Formula Editor:

If the predefined settings are not working as expected, use the Formula Editor to create a custom calculation for the average.

 

Example:
scss
Copy code
Average_Profit = SUM(Profit) / COUNT_DISTINCT([Region])
Replace [Region] with the appropriate dimension for your dataset.
Test in a Table Visualization:

Add a table to your story and include the calculated measure alongside Region.
Check if the calculated measure is showing the correct average for each region.
Refresh Data:

Refresh the model or data source to ensure the calculated measure is updated correctly.
Check for Story-Level Overrides:

Look for any story-level custom aggregations that might be overriding your calculated measure settings.

 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Build and Run Multi-Tenant SaaS Solutions on the SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi Aleksei,

let me start to sort out the terms multi-tenant, multi-target and multi-backend:

Multi-tenancy refers to a solution architecture and deployment model tailored to SaaS solutions that you want to provide to many customers or consumers. It means you deploy your solution once with one database and one runtime, and then provide your solution to customers as subscriptions (tenants). In our reference example we are using the SAP Multi-Tenancy Extension (MTX) module, which is well-integrated with CAP and BTP services out-of-the-box.

A multi-target application is logically a single application comprised of multiple parts created with different technologies, which share the same lifecycle. In our reference example we are using the Multi-Target Application module (MTA) because is provides a standardized way to model the application structure and its dependencies. The MTA deployment service provides a very high degree of automation and ensures an automated, parallel and deterministic deployment/un-deployment, it automatically provisions required BTP services, it automatically creates routes, domains, and technical users, and it ensures a transactional consistency of deployments and recoverability, ... 

Multi-backend simply means that we designed our reference application as ERP-agnostic solution such that every subscription of the multi-tenant app can be connected to ERP system of the customer, regardless if it is an S/4HANA Cloud system, a B1 system, a ByD system or any other ERP.

Regarding the nature of the partner reference application: Yes it is definitely not designed as Hello World example, but rather explains how BTP services can be used and orchestrated to deliver enterprise-ready solutions that fit to the SAP standard in a scalable model. To get started with CAP, I would recommend the CAPire documentation with the "bookshop"-example. The Discovery Center provides you with a comprehensive catalog of use case specific missions. And last but not least I would recommend the learning journey Building side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP as a good starting point as well.

entry.title: Profitability report by the Profit center group
entry.content:
Hi All,

One of my client is having the requirement to generate the profitability report by the profit center group.

since we don't have the standard character of profit center group. as selection input.

Regards,

Santhosha BH

entry.title: DR ACTIVATION IN AN S/4 RISE ALREADY ACTIVE
entry.content:
Hi,

I need to start a S2D just for DR in an RISE contract that already have S/4HANA running.

To do that, all I need to do is:

  • Create a COF FORM of the current S/4HANA PRIV RISE, selecting "S/4HANA EM" in the ADDITIONAL SOLUTIONS", with all "USER INFORMATION" and "SOLUTION INFORMATION"
  • Add all currect connectivity information in "CONNECTIVITY INFORMATION", including all DR information.

Is that correct?

Thank you.
Maurício.

entry.title: Re: BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_CREATE_SLS giving random result
entry.content:
Could you paste the code where SALES_ORDER_ITEMS is filled? (Did you specify delivery quantities)

Did you try to explicitly close the RFC connections with RFC_CONNECTION_CLOSE between call (check with SM04)

entry.title: Re: Automate updates to my dashboard imported from an excel
entry.content:
Hi,

Ok William, would you have a guide to recommend how to do what is suggested? please

Best regards

entry.title: Unable to fetch KBETR field in price condition using formula routine
entry.content:
We have (zppt price per )tonne which is header condition type also we have ZB00 is base price item condition, we will enter manually zppt price at header and we have to calculate base price condition amount in KBETR ,we have created 918 condition value formula routine , we unable fetch zb00 condition rate in KBETR field, we are getting calculated XKWERT field i.e condition value, unable fetch KBETR, please suggest solution.

data : lt_pack TYPE TABLE OF zpack, wa_pack TYPE zpack.

data : lt_marm TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF marm, wa_marm type marm.

data : lt_marm1 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF marm, wa_marm1 type marm.

data : lt_prcd TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF prcd_elements, wa_prcd type prcd_elements.

*DATA : ZB001 TYPE p DECIMALS 3.

DATA : ZB001 TYPE decfloat34.

DATA : ZB002 TYPE KWERT.

DATA : ZB003 TYPE KWERT.

data : lv_conversion TYPE f.

SELECT * FROM zpack INTO TABLE lt_pack WHERE matnr = komp-matnr and WERKS = komp-werks and meins = komp-meins.

SORT lt_pack DESCENDING by edate. SELECT * FROM marm INTO TABLE lt_marm where matnr = komp-matnr and meinh = 'TO'.

SELECT * from prcd_elements into table lt_prcd where knumv = komk-knumv and kschl = 'ZPPT'.

READ TABLE lt_marm into wa_marm INDEX 1. READ TABLE lt_pack into wa_pack INDEX 1. READ TABLE lt_prcd into wa_prcd with key kschl = 'ZPPT' kposn = komp-kposn.

ZB001 = ( wa_marm-umren / wa_marm-umrez ) .

IF KOMV-KSCHL = 'ZPPT'.

ZB002 = ZB001 * KOMV-KBETR .

ZB003 = ZB002 + wa_pack-rate.

xkwert = ZB003 * KOMP-MGAME / 1000 . ELSEIF

KOMV-KSCHL = 'ZB00'.

ZB002 = KOMV-kbetr.

xkwert = ZB002 * KOMP-MGAME / 1000 . ENDIF.

*ZB003 = ZB002 + wa_pack-rate.

xkwert = ZB003 KOMP-MGAME / 1000     above code is written in condition value formula routine

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content: Yukon BPA amount is not changed for the year 2025. The effective date in T511K constant from 2024 is 15,969.00 and no change after that. Is SAP going to release a separate note for this ?

entry.title: Re: Writing file to Azure Blob from CPI !
entry.content: is it possible to export from BTP IS and import to CPI CF?

entry.title: Error HUGENERAL066 in LI20 During Inventory Write-Off
entry.content:
Hello All,

We are encountering an issue during the Physical Inventory (PI) process in transaction LI20 when attempting to write off inventory. The system generates error HUGENERAL066:

"Quantity 0 (not packed) is less than the requested quantity 4."

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Go into transaction LI20.
  2. Enter the inventory record and Warehouse, Variance in %: 10
  3. Click on “Write Off”.
  4. The following error message appears:
    • Quantity 0 (not packed) is less than the requested quantity 4.
    • Message No.: HUGENERAL066

Do you have any suggestions on how to resolve this error? Is there a configuration, process step, or master data issue that could be causing the discrepancy?

Thank you in advance for your support and insights.

entry.title: Re: LTI Template in SuccessFactors Compensation
entry.content:
Hi @satnaik18 

Please find more information below:

Screenshots and leading practice design of LTI template. 

Important Notes About Stock Planning Templates

Creating Stock Value Tables

Creating Stock Factor Tables

Creating Stock Participation Guideline Tables

 

 

Other things worth mentioning: we cannot move around column freely in the stock tab as we can in the Compensation tab. Any custom column we add will always show to the right of the stock recommendation columns. Which is often the reason why we end up using a dedicated Compensation template or a group of columns inside the Compensation tab to do stock planning (since we can do pretty much the same thing using lookup tables).

I have done 20+ implementations in the last 10 years and I configured the stock tab only once.

Hope this helps.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Compensation story report average
entry.content:
Hi @Jen_Cooper 

I am not sure on this one but I just reached out to my colleagues from the reporting team.

All the best,
Xavier

entry.title: Re: How to get UUID of WBS element using API in s4hana cloud
entry.content:
Hello, could you find a solution for this? I am in exactly the same spot as you.
Thanks

entry.title: Re: Is SQL Anywhere viable and sustainable
entry.content:
As to the EOMM 2028 date, see the following question and Jason Noack's answer:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/no-sql-anywhere-ebf-in-5-months/qaq-p/13837725

According to that, SQL Anywhere is at least supported till 2028-12-31, and that date has been extended several times in the past (from 12/2023 to 12/2025 to 12/2027, IIRC...).

That being said, I strongly share the impression that less and less effort is put into enhancing the product. Nevertheless, for our needs and from a developer and part-time-DBA point of view, it does so many things much better than MS SQL Server, which we also use...

entry.title: Re: Group Valuation - 5W2
entry.content:
Hi sanne_larsen

To effectively utilize the 5W2 scope item, please activate the following scope items:

  • 5HP - Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer
  • 5D2 - Advanced Intercompany Sales
  • 5MQ - Sell from Stock with Valuated Stock in Transit

Find more details:

SAP Note 3100892 - Group Valuation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

SAP Help Portal links:

      • Group Valuation (5W2)
      • Advanced Intercompany Sales (5D2)
      • Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer (5HP)
      • Sell from Stock with Valuated Stock in Transit (5MQ)

Best regards

Edna

entry.title: Re: Unable to delete and update/Insert at once using 'cl_md_bp_maintain=>maintain' class
entry.content:
Thanks.

Insert operation is perfectly working fine for me as well. I need help for Update and Delete operations that too most importantly for handling multiple entries for Email and Telephone. Iam passing U and D as tasks for Update and Delete respectively, rest part of the code is exactly same as mentioned in the blog.

entry.title: Re: 3244147 - How to retrieve the classification data fro...
entry.content:
Hello @Tom1-2-3 

 

The KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3502310 outlines 2 ways to avoid getting the error :

 

Check the application component(business area) for a CDS view of the associated Data Source by steps below:

  1. Login to the Custom CDS view app, Select the scenario the Data Extraction.
  2. Click on add to add the primary data source using F4 help, kindly go to the option settings and enable the filter for component. 
  3. The field component will provide the application area to which the CDS view belongs.

There is a workaround: 

  1. Create a CDS view as scenario Cube or Dimension, with the same data sources.
  2. Create Data Extraction based on the created custom CDS view.

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: Enable Now Producer not playing
entry.content: And yes, please send me the new version, although, I might be looking into using Producer for Wflow - instead of Live review

entry.title: Re: Unable to delete and update/Insert at once using 'cl_md_bp_maintain=>maintain' class
entry.content: Search the SAP notes related to your query e.g. 3366047 - Telephone number under communication not updated with CL_MD_BP_MAINTAIN in S/4HANA - SAP for Me
